nodes.x: Judge me, O Lord; for I have walked in mine integrity: I have trusted also in the Lord; therefore I shall not slide.
But as for me, I will walk in mine integrity: redeem me, and be merciful unto me.
shall judge the people: judge me, O Lord, according to my righteou$ne$$ t and according to mine integrity that is
The Lord shall judge the people: judge me, O Lord, according to my righteousness, and according to mine integrity that is in me.
I am the Lord your God; walk in my statutes, and keep my judgments, and do them;
Let me be weighed in an even balance, that God may know mine integrity.
Examine me, O Lord, and prove me; try my reins and my heart.
I the Lord search the heart, I try the reins, even to give every man according to his ways, and according to the fruit of his doings.
Let us search and try our ways, and turn again to the Lord.
Make haste to help me, O Lord my salvation.
And seek the face of the Lord always, that in patience ye may possess your souls, and ye shall have eternal life.
Seek the Lord and his strength, seek his face continually.
For thy lovingkindness is before mine eyes: and I have walked in thy truth.
But mine eyes are unto thee, O God the Lord: in thee is my trust; leave not my soul destitute.
For mine eyes have seen thy salvation,
Mine eyes fail for thy word, saying, When wilt thou comfort me?
I have chosen the way of truth: thy judgments have I laid before me.
Mine eyes fail for thy salvation, and for the word of thy righteousness.
I have not sat with vain persons, neither will I go in with dissemblers.
Have ye suffered so many things in vain? if it be yet in vain.
Surely every man walketh in a vain shew: surely they are disquieted in vain: he heapeth up riches, and knoweth not who shall gather them.
I hate vain thoughts: but thy law do I love.
Shall vain words have an end? or what emboldeneth thee that thou answerest?
I have hated the congregation of evil doers; and will not sit with the wicked.
I was almost in all evil in the midst of the congregation and assembly.
I the Lord have said, I will surely do it unto all this evil congregation, that are gathered together against me: in this wilderness they shall be consumed, and there they shall die.
Whose hatred is covered by deceit, his wickedness shall be shewed before the whole congregation.
And finally, all ye that will persist in your wickedness, I say unto you that these are they who shall be hewn down and cast into the fire except they speedily repent.
I will wash mine hands in innocency: so will I compass thine altar, O Lord:
And your altars shall be desolate, and your images shall be broken: and I will cast down your slain men before your idols.

Yet I will send my servants unto thee to morrow about this time, and they shall search thine house, and the houses of thy servants; and it shall be, that whatsoever is pleasant in thine eyes, they shall put it in their hand, and take it away.
And I will lay the dead carcases of the children of Israel before their idols; and I will scatter your bones round about your altars.
¶ Then will I sprinkle clean water upon you, and ye shall be clean: from all your filthiness, and from all your idols, will I cleanse you.
That I may publish with the voice of thanksgiving, and tell of all thy wondrous works.
I will praise the name of God with a song, and will magnify him with thanksgiving.
Because I will publish the name of the Lord: ascribe ye greatness unto our God.
And, behold, they will publish this, and Satan will harden the hearts of the people to stir them up to anger against you, that they will not believe my words.
Be careful for nothing; but in every thing by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known unto God.
Lord, I have loved the habitation of thy house, and the place where thine honour dwelleth.
But I have built an house of habitation for thee, and a place for thy dwelling for ever.
I have surely built thee an house to dwell in, a settled place for thee to abide in for ever.

I pray thee, forgive the trespass of thine handmaid: for the Lord will certainly make my lord a sure house; because my lord fighteth the battles of the Lord, and evil hath not been found in thee all thy days.
Gather not my soul with sinners, nor my life with bloody men:
(For that righteous man dwelling among them, in seeing and hearing, vexed his righteous soul from day to day with their unlawful deeds;)
Awake, my soul! No longer droop in sin. Rejoice, O my heart, and give place no more for the enemy of my soul.
And I will bring distress upon men, that they shall walk like blind men, because they have sinned against the Lord: and their blood shall be poured out as dust, and their flesh as the dung.
But behold, I am consigned that these are my days, and that my soul shall be filled with sorrow because of this the wickedness of my brethren.
In whose hands is mischief, and their right hand is full of bribes.
¶ Withhold not good from them to whom it is due, when it is in the power of thine hand to do it.
They draw nigh that follow after mischief: they are far from thy law.
They conceive mischief, and bring forth vanity, and their belly prepareth deceit.
While they promise them liberty, they themselves are the servants of corruption: for of whom a man is overcome, of the same is he brought in bondage.
But as for me, I will walk in mine integrity: redeem me, and be merciful unto me.
God forbid that I should justify you: till I die I will not remove mine integrity from me.
Judge me, O Lord; for I have walked in mine integrity: I have trusted also in the Lord; therefore I shall not slide.
And as for me, thou upholdest me in mine integrity, and settest me before thy face for ever.
My foot standeth in an even place: in the congregations will I bless the Lord.
And your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace;
Praise ye the Lord. I will praise the Lord with my whole heart, in the assembly of the upright, and in the congregation.

Stand, therefore, having your loins girt about with truth, having on the breastplate of righteousness, and your feet shod with the preparation of the gospel of peace, which I have sent mine angels to commit unto you;

foot well, the foot wishes the head well, and will walk to get food for the head and stomach, and they are united, and we shall become more and more united. And I pray that the Lord will pour out his grace on his sons and daughters, and I pray the Saints to improve upon it until we are sanctified. God bless you! Amen.
